perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shared under this Agreement may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to other employees on a n eed to know basis, with written consent from the Disclosing Party, in connection with the Purpose, and who shall protect the Confidential Information in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shared under this Agreement may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to other employees in connection with the Purpose, and who shall protect the Confidential Information in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'with written consent from the Disclosing Party' introduces a contradiction. While the changed text permits disclosure to employees, earlier sections establish that all Confidential Information must be treated with the utmost secrecy. Without the requirement for written consent, it becomes ambiguous whether the Disclosing Party retains control over who accesses the Confidential Information, contradicting the implied intent of stringent confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All such Confidential Information shared under this Agreement shall be used by the Parties exclusively for the Purpose and neither Party shall disclose or otherwise use the Confidential Information for any other purpose or in any other manner without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'written consent from the Disclosing Party' before the Receiving Party discloses Confidential Information to its employees. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that neither party shall disclose or use Confidential Information without the 'prior written approval of the Disclosing Party'. The omission creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the control and approval process for disclosing confidential information, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Confidential Inform ation in the same manner as it would protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Confidential Information using reasonable measures.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the original standard ('same manner as it would protect its own confidential information') with a vague 'reasonable measures' creates a contradiction. The original text implies a high level of protection, aligned with the Receiving Party's own sensitive data handling. The altered text introduces ambiguity and lowers the standard, potentially conflicting with the expectation set in other clauses that Confidential Information will be treated with exceptional care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the req uest of the Disclosing Party or upon the termination of this Agreement, return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, including drawings, documents, reports and other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received by the Receiv ing Party pursuant to this Agreement, together with all copies and reproductions thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to protect Confidential Information in the same manner as its own confidential information, implying a high standard of care. The changed text replaces this with 'reasonable measures,' which is a vague and potentially lower standard. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to return 'all Confidential Information' upon request or termination. The vagueness of 'reasonable measures' in the changed text now contradicts the expectation that all confidential information will be protected to a high standard and available for return, as originally intended.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees not to issue or release for publication any arti cles or advertising or publicity matter relating to this Agreement which mention or imply the name of the Disclosing Party any of its affiliates, or subject matter hereof, unless prior written consent is granted by the Disclosing Party subject only to Clau se 7. The Receiving Party shall make such amendments to any such press release or public statement as are reasonably requested by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees not to issue or release for publication any articles or advertising or publicity matter relating to this Agreement which mention or imply the name of the Disclosing Party any of its affiliates, or subject matter hereof. The Receiving Party shall make such amendments to any such press release or public statement as are reasonably requested by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'unless prior written consent is granted by the Disclosing Party subject only to Clause 7' clause, the modified text creates a contradiction. Clause 7 allows disclosure as required by law. The omitted text provided a balance by requiring consent for other disclosures. Now, the contract appears to prohibit all public statements, which could conflict with the Receiving Party's legal obligations under Clause 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, Confidential Information may be disclosed as required by applicable law, regulations or governmental procedure , provided the Receiving Party notifies the Disclosing Party prior to such d isclosure, unless prohibited by law, so as to afford the Disclosing Non -Disclosure Agreement Template (India) | OS v.1 | November 2016 2 Party reasonable opportunity to object or seek an appropriate protective order with respect to such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for disclosures with prior written consent from the Disclosing Party, subject to Clause 7 which permitted legally required disclosures. The changed text removes the consent clause, potentially prohibiting all public statements. This creates a direct contradiction with Clause 7, which requires disclosure when mandated by law. The changed text makes the contract appear to forbid something that Clause 7 requires, creating a strong and irreconcilable contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The fact that Confidential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party shall not be deemed to constitut e any representation, warranty or inducement by the Disclosing Party o f any kind (including of its accuracy or correctness) with respect to the Confidential Information, including without limitation, which such use will not infringe on intellectual property rights of any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The fact that Confidential Information is disclosed to the Receiving Party shall not be deemed to constitute any representation, warranty or inducement by the Disclosing Party of any kind with respect to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase '(including of its accuracy or correctness) with respect to the Confidential Information, including without limitation, which such use will not infringe on intellectual property rights of any third party' creates an in-text contradiction because, without this disclaimer, the Disclosing Party could be held liable for inaccuracies or IP infringements related to the Confidential Information, which contradicts the intention of the clause, which is to limit liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information ” shall mean and include all non -public information, written or oral, disclosed, directly or indirectly, through any means of communication or observation (including oral, graphic, written or electronic form) by the Disclosing Party or any of its affiliates or representatives to or for the benefit of the Receiving Party from the Effective Date, irrespective of whether such inform ation: (a) has been specifically marked as “confidential” at the time of disclosure; (b) is treated as proprietary information by the Disclosing Party; or (c) is owned or developed by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the phrase '(including of its accuracy or correctness) with respect to the Confidential Information, including without limitation, which such use will not infringe on intellectual property rights of any third party' from the original text creates a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text, without this disclaimer, implies that the Disclosing Party could be held liable for inaccuracies or IP infringements related to the Confidential Information. This directly conflicts with the intention to limit liability. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, encompassing a wide range of non-public information, regardless of whether it's marked confidential or owned by the Disclosing Party. The original disclaimer protected the Disclosing party when disclosing that Confidential Information, but the changed text exposes the Disclosing party to more liabilities. This is a strong contradiction that would cause confusion about each party's liabilities, therefore it is a YES.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall terminate on the delivery of written notice of termination from either Party; provided, however, that the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of [__] years from the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall terminate on the delivery of written notice of termination from either Party; provided, however, that the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall remain in effect.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'for a period of [__] years from the date of termination' creates a contradiction regarding the agreement's duration. While the text states obligations remain in effect post-termination, it omits the specific duration. This implies indefinite confidentiality obligations, which is highly unusual and might be challenged as unreasonable, potentially conflicting with legal principles that require reasonable limits on such obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All such Confidential Information shared under this Agreement shall be used by the Parties exclusively for the Purpose and neither Party shall disclose or otherwise use th e Confidential Information for any other purpose or in any other manner without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a duration for the Receiving Party's obligations after termination ('for a period of [__] years from the date of termination'). The changed text removes this duration, making the obligations potentially indefinite. This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a finite duration for the use and non-disclosure of Confidential Information ('All such Confidential Information shared under this Agreement shall be used by the Parties exclusively for the Purpose...'). The indefinite nature introduced by the change creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability issues, as it conflicts with the reasonable limitations expected in confidentiality agreements, aligning with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.
